Name: dynamic_jacobian_layer_skipping
Title: Dynamic Jacobian Layer-Skipping for Latency-Aware Inference Optimization
Short Hypothesis: By leveraging Jacobian information to dynamically skip layers during inference, we can achieve significant reductions in latency without compromising accuracy, thereby establishing a new paradigm for latency-aware machine learning inference.
Related Work: Existing works, such as Spectral Quantization Routing and Training-Free Geometric Interference Cancellation, focus on static strategies for optimizing inference latency. In contrast, our proposal introduces a dynamic, real-time mechanism that adapts the inference pathway based on Jacobian characteristics, which has not been extensively explored in the context of layer-skipping.
Abstract: In the pursuit of optimizing machine learning inference under strict latency constraints, we propose a novel approach that utilizes Jacobian information to dynamically skip layers during the inference process. This method is grounded in the premise that certain layers may contribute disproportionately to latency without yielding commensurate accuracy benefits. Our framework computes the Jacobian matrix on-the-fly, assessing the sensitivity of output predictions to input perturbations. Based on this analysis, the model intelligently decides which layers to skip, thus minimizing the overall computation while maintaining performance. We will validate our approach across various datasets and architectures, measuring throughput, accuracy, and actual wall-clock time to establish the efficacy of this dynamic layer-skipping strategy. The proposed method is expected to set a new standard for resource-efficient inference, particularly in latency-critical applications.
Experiments: 1. Baseline Evaluation: Measure inference latency and accuracy of standard architectures (e.g., ResNet, Transformer) without layer skipping.
2. Jacobian Computation: Implement a mechanism to compute the Jacobian matrix during inference for a subset of test cases.
3. Dynamic Layer-Skipping: Create a layer-skipping heuristic based on Jacobian sensitivity and evaluate its impact on latency and accuracy across multiple architectures.
4. Performance Comparison: Compare the throughput-accuracy Pareto frontier of dynamic layer-skipping against traditional inference methods and static layer-skipping techniques.
Evaluation Metrics: Latency (in ms), accuracy (percentage), and throughput (samples/sec) will be the primary metrics.
Risk Factors and Limitations: 1. The dynamic nature of layer skipping may introduce variability in performance that could affect reproducibility. 2. The computational overhead of Jacobian computation could negate latency benefits in certain scenarios. 3. The method's efficiency may be architecture-dependent, limiting generalizability across models.
